cards.front: What first-order transfer function is studied in the note?
Where is the pole of H(s)=K/(s+B)?
cards.back: At s=-B on the negative real axis.

cards.front: What is H(omega) for the first-order system?
What is the magnitude response of H(s)=K/(s+B)?
What is the phase response of H(s)=K/(s+B)?
What is the low-frequency magnitude limit?
cards.back: As omega goes to 0, |H(omega)| goes to K/B.

cards.front: What is the high-frequency phase limit?
cards.back: As omega goes to infinity, angle H(omega) goes to -90 degrees.
The magnitude becomes K/(sqrt(2)B) and the phase becomes -45 degrees.

cards.front: Why is B called the 3 dB bandwidth?
cards.back: Because the magnitude at omega=B is 3 dB below the low-frequency level.

cards.front: What filter type does the first-order example resemble?
